zh.Merbes-le-Château, Hainaut, Belgique: 比利時海瑙特梅貝勒-a奧
zh.lifeSummary: 當Elisabeth DE MERBES LE CHÂTEAU於1195年出生在比利時海瑙特梅貝勒-a奧時，她的父親Baudouin IV DE MERBES LE CHATEAU40歲，母親Marie DE BOUSTAINE26歲。她於1219年與Nicolas II de Barbancon結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1220年在法國享年25歲。

zh-hans.Merbes-le-Château, Hainaut, Belgique: 比利时海瑙特梅貝勒-a奧
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elisabeth DE MERBES LE CHÂTEAU于1195年出生在比利时海瑙特梅貝勒-a奧时，她的父亲Baudouin IV DE MERBES LE CHATEAU40岁，母亲Marie DE BOUSTAINE26岁。她于1219年与Nicolas II de Barbancon结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1220年在法国享年25岁。

nameMeanings.name: DE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: MERBES
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: North German and Dutch: variant of   Mertens  .

variants.name: Mertens
meanings: North German, Dutch, and Flemish: patronymic from a variant of the personal name   Martin  . The surname of Flemish and German origin is also found in the Walloon part of Belgium.

nameMeanings.name: LE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: CHÂTEAU
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Thierry, Francois, Michel.
French (Château):
from Old French chastel (French château) ‘castle, fortified building’, a topographic name for someone who lived in or near a castle, or a metonymic occupational name for someone who worked in one.

variants.name: Chatel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Celine, Emile, Gilles, Jacques, Lucien.
French (also Châtel):
habitational name from (Le) Chatel or (Le) Châtel, names of several places in various parts of France, from Old French chastel ‘castle, fortified building’.

variants.name: Duchateau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Andree, Armand, Philippe.

French (Duchâteau); Walloon and Flemish (rarely Duchâteau): topographic name, with fused French preposition and definite article du ‘from the’, from Old French chastel (French château) ‘castle, fortified building’ (see Chateau ), or a habitational name denoting someone from Le Château, the name of several places in various parts of France and in Belgium (Wallonia) named with château.

nameMeanings.name: Elisabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

variants.name: Elizabeth
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Elisabeth DE MERBES LE CHÂTEAU was born in 1195, in Merbes-le-Château, Hainaut, Belgium, her father, Baudouin IV DE MERBES LE CHATEAU, was 40 and her mother, Marie DE BOUSTAINE, was 26. She married Nicolas II de Barbancon in 1219. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1220, in France, at the age of 25.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elisabeth DE MERBES LE CHÂTEAU est née en 1195, à Merbes-le-Château, Hainaut, Belgique, son père, Baudouin IV DE MERBES LE CHATEAU, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Marie DE BOUSTAINE, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé Nicolas II de Barbancon en 1219. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée en 1220, à France, à l’âge de 25 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elisabeth DE MERBES LE CHÂTEAU 1195 in Merbes-le-Château, Hennegau, Belgien geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Baudouin IV DE MERBES LE CHATEAU 40 und ihre Mutter Marie DE BOUSTAINE 26. Sie hat 1219 Nicolas II de Barbancon geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1220 im Alter von 25 Jahren in Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elisabeth DE MERBES LE CHÂTEAU è nata il nel 1195 nel Merbes-le-Château, Hainaut, Belgio, sua padre, Baudouin IV DE MERBES LE CHATEAU, aveva 40 anni e sua madre, Marie DE BOUSTAINE, aveva 26 anni. Lei sposato Nicolas II de Barbancon nel 1219. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1220, nel Francia, all'età di 25 anni.

ja.Merbes-le-Château, Hainaut, Belgique: ベルギーヘイナトメルベス-レ-チaテアウ
ja.lifeSummary: Elisabeth DE MERBES LE CHÂTEAUが1195年にベルギーヘイナトメルベス-レ-チaテアウで生まれた時，父Baudouin IV DE MERBES LE CHATEAUは40歳，母Marie DE BOUSTAINEは26歳であった。1219年にNicolas II de Barbanconと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1220年にフランスで25歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elisabeth DE MERBES LE CHÂTEAU(이)가 1195년 벨기에 하인오트 머베스-레-cha테오에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Baudouin IV DE MERBES LE CHATEAU(은)는 40세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Marie DE BOUSTAINE(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 1219년 Nicolas II de Barbancon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1220년 프랑스에서 25세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elisabeth DE MERBES LE CHÂTEAU nasceu em 1195, em Merbes-le-Château, Hainaut, Bélgica, seu pai, Baudouin IV DE MERBES LE CHATEAU, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Marie DE BOUSTAINE, tinha 26 anos. Ela casou-se com Nicolas II de Barbancon em 1219. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 1220, em França, com 25 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elisabeth DE MERBES LE CHÂTEAU родилась в 1195 г. в местности мирбес-ле-чaто, ханаут, Бельгия, её отцу по имени Baudouin IV DE MERBES LE CHATEAU было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Marie DE BOUSTAINE, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Nicolas II de Barbancon в 1219 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1220 г. в местности Франция в возрасте 25 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elisabeth DE MERBES LE CHÂTEAU nació en 1195, en Merbes-le-Château, Hainaut, Bélgica, su papá, Baudouin IV DE MERBES LE CHATEAU, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Marie DE BOUSTAINE, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Nicolas II de Barbancon en 1219. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1220, en Francia, a la edad de 25 años.
